- Bachelor’s degree in Industrial Engineering, Manufacturing Engineering, or a related field; Master’s degree preferred.
- 5-10 years of progressive industrial engineering experience within a manufacturing or operations environment, ideally in electronics or advanced technology hardware production.
- Strong proficiency with Lean Manufacturing, Six Sigma (Green Belt or higher certification preferred), and continuous improvement methodologies.
- Hands-on experience with simulation software such as Arena, FlexSim, or ProModel for manufacturing system analysis.
- Expertise in data analysis and visualization tools including SQL, Python (pandas, numpy), Power BI, or Tableau.

This role offers a unique opportunity to significantly impact manufacturing operations through innovation and data-driven decision-making at the forefront of industrial IoT technology. We look forward to your application.

You will collaborate closely with cross-functional teams including supply chain planners, production managers, and IT to develop and deploy optimization algorithms and simulation models that support strategic decision-making. About the Role
We are seeking a motivated and detail-oriented Junior Application Security Engineer to join our security team. This is an excellent opportunity for a recent graduate or early-career professional passionate about application security and eager to grow their skills in a fast-paced, cloud-native environment. You will work closely with senior engineers, developers, and product teams to help identify and remediate security vulnerabilities in modern software applications. The ideal candidate has a foundational understanding of software development, security principles, and cloud technologies, and is excited to learn and apply industry best practices in application security.

Responsibilities
- Assist in the identification, analysis, and remediation of application security vulnerabilities using static application security testing (SAST), dynamic application security testing (DAST), and software composition analysis (SCA) tools.
- Collaborate with development teams to integrate security tooling into CI/CD pipelines, ensuring security checks are automated and scalable.
- Participate in security code reviews and threat modeling exercises to support secure software development lifecycle (SSDLC) initiatives.
- Monitor and triage security alerts from internal tools and third-party platforms, escalating critical issues as needed.
- Contribute to the creation and maintenance of security documentation, guidelines, and best practices for developers.
- Support incident response efforts related to application security incidents under the guidance of senior team members.

- Exposure to application security tools such as [Company], SonarQube, OWASP ZAP, or similar platforms.

About the Role
We are seeking an experienced Director of Clinical Data Science to lead and expand our team of data scientists dedicated to transforming cancer care through real-world evidence. In this role, you will drive the strategic vision and execution of clinical data science initiatives, collaborating closely with cross-functional teams including oncology research, product, engineering, and regulatory affairs. You will leverage advanced analytical methods and state-of-the-art technology platforms to generate insights from large-scale, longitudinal clinical datasets. This leadership position requires a deep understanding of oncology, real-world data (RWD) sources, and the regulatory landscape, alongside a proven track record managing high-performing teams in a fast-paced, mission-driven environment.

Responsibilities
- Lead and mentor a multidisciplinary team of clinical data scientists, biostatisticians, and machine learning engineers to deliver impactful analytical solutions.
- Develop and implement innovative methodologies for extracting and interpreting real-world oncology data, including electronic health records (EHR), claims data, and genomic datasets.
- Collaborate with clinical and regulatory stakeholders to design and validate real-world evidence (RWE) studies that support drug development, market access, and post-market surveillance.
- Drive adoption of scalable data science workflows using cloud computing platforms such as AWS or GCP, and leverage distributed processing frameworks like Apache Spark.
- Oversee the application of advanced statistical techniques, causal inference models, and machine learning algorithms tailored for clinical endpoints and patient outcome predictions.
- Establish best practices for data quality, governance, and reproducibility aligned with FDA and EMA regulatory standards.
- Partner with engineering teams to operationalize predictive models and analytics pipelines within production-grade software applications.
- Represent the clinical data science function in cross-organizational strategic discussions and external scientific forums.

Requirements
- 10+ years of experience in clinical data science, biostatistics, or related quantitative roles within healthcare, biotech, or pharmaceutical industries.
- Proven leadership experience managing data science teams at the staff/principal or director level.
- Expertise in oncology clinical trial design, real-world data analytics, and regulatory frameworks for RWE (e.g., FDA’s RWE guidance).
- Proficiency in programming languages and tools such as Python (pandas, scikit-learn), R (survival, nlme), SQL, and experience with cloud platforms (AWS, GCP, or Azure).
- Strong background in epidemiology, biostatistics, or computational biology with advanced degrees (PhD or equivalent preferred).
- Familiarity with healthcare data standards and ontologies including OMOP CDM, HL7 FHIR, and ICD-10 coding.
- Experience with machine learning frameworks (TensorFlow, PyTorch) and statistical modeling of time-to-event data and causal inference techniques (e.g., propensity score matching, instrumental variables).
- Excellent communication skills to translate complex analyses into actionable insights for both scientific and non-technical audiences.

Nice to Have

- Knowledge of software development lifecycle (SDLC) and MLOps practices for deploying clinical AI solutions.
- Publications or presentations at leading conferences in oncology, biostatistics, or clinical informatics.
- Familiarity with natural language processing (NLP) techniques applied to unstructured clinical notes.

Sr Data Engineer - GE07BE
We’re determined to make a difference and are proud to be an insurance company that goes well beyond coverages and policies. Working here means having every opportunity to achieve your goals – and to help others accomplish theirs, too. Join our team as we help shape the future.

Overview of Role
The Sr. Applied Analytics Engineer drives the design, engineering, and delivery of AI-powered analytics solutions within [Company]'s Applied Analytics function. This role sits at the intersection of Artificial Intelligence, Business Intelligence, and production-grade engineering — building and operationalizing conversational AI agents and agentic analytics workflows. Responsible for designing sophisticated AI solutions that enable business users to effortlessly explore complex insurance data and derive actionable insights in plain English, while also owning the engineering lifecycle that makes those solutions reliable, scalable, and maintainable in production. This position is for a builder who is passionate about transforming data exploration, democratizing access to insights, and delivering sustainable, production-ready AI capabilities within a dynamic enterprise environment.
Our Core Values
We build AI solutions, not models. We are thoughtful in supporting the end-to-end business problem, with an eye to systems design.
We are trusted and transparent. We collaborate tightly with our partners and are mindful of their capacity to absorb change.
We provide assets that are safe to buy. Our products are delivered with a full monitoring solution to ensure our products continue to deliver as expected.
We will earn the right to influence. With humble confidence, we listen carefully to learn from our customers and become partners in problem solving.
We are practical and evolutional. We first deliver a minimally viable product and over time expand its sophistication based on feedback.
Primary Job Responsibilities
Design \& Deliver Conversational AI Solutions: Build advanced generative and agentic AI solutions with a strong emphasis on conversational interfaces. This includes RAG pipelines, intelligent chat/assistant systems, and autonomous analytics workflows — leveraging a fit-for-purpose toolkit from structured retrieval to sophisticated agentic workflows.
Business Intelligence \& Data Integration: Partner with data engineering and BI teams to ensure AI solutions consume and surface accurate, consistent, and interpretable insurance data — creating a unified and business-friendly view for AI agents and business intelligence tools.
End-to-End Solution Engineering \& MLOps: Own the AI solution lifecycle from problem framing through production deployment and ongoing operations. This encompasses data preparation, model development, rigorous evaluation, CI/CD pipeline development, orchestration, observability, safety filters/guardrails, and comprehensive rollback plans. Design, develop, and maintain Models as Service, adopting and promoting MLOps best practices across the analytics engineering team.
Cloud Infrastructure \& Deployment: Deliver production AI/ML solutions across AWS and GCP cloud environments. Develop repeatable architectural patterns using Infrastructure as Code (CloudFormation, Terraform, or similar), containerization (Docker, Kubernetes), and cloud-native ML platforms to ensure scalable, secure, and reproducible deployments.
Required Skills \& Qualifications
Business Intelligence \& Data Literacy: Strong understanding of BI principles, tools, and data warehouse/data lake environments. Ability to work with and interpret complex insurance data structures to support AI-driven analytics.
Conversational AI System Development: Experience in designing, developing, and deploying conversational AI systems, including RAG pipelines, chatbots, virtual assistants, and intelligent agents.
Python \& Data Proficiency: Proficiency with pandas and numpy. Strong SQL skills for complex data exploration, feature engineering, and knowledge preparation.
End-to-End Modeling \& Deployment Lifecycle: Experience with requirements gathering, experiment design, offline evaluation, production deployment, and post-production monitoring/validation of AI and BI solutions.
MLOps \& CI/CD: Experience building and maintaining CI/CD pipelines (Jenkins, GitHub Actions, or equivalent) for ML model deployment. Familiarity with experiment tracking, model registries, and evaluation gates.
Cloud Platforms \& Infrastructure: Development experience using the AWS and GCP suites of tools. Familiarity with SageMaker, Vertex AI, Bedrock, or Azure AI Services. Experience with Infrastructure as Code (CloudFormation, Terraform, or similar).
Data Pipeline Architecture: Experience with solution design and architecture of data pipelines, including workflow automation platforms (Apache Airflow, Autosys, or similar) and big data technologies (Spark, Hive, [Company], BigQuery, or Redshift).
Core Algorithms \& Architectures: Solid understanding of natural language processing methods and generative AI architectures.
NLP \& Generative AI Fundamentals: In-depth knowledge of embeddings, hybrid\+dense retrieval, advanced chunking strategies, prompt engineering, structured outputs, and the fundamentals of agent/tool-use for conversational AI.
Version Control \& Engineering Practices: Expert-level Git experience including GitHub Actions. Proficient with Unix; experience with reproducible notebooks/pipelines. Strong object-oriented development skills in Python. Container/cloud literacy (Docker, Kubernetes).
Communication: Exceptional ability to explain complex design trade-offs, evaluation results, and risks to both highly technical and non-technical business audiences, translating directly into clear business outcomes and strategies.
Insurance Domain Knowledge: Foundational understanding of insurance products and processes, with emphasis on operations, service, and workflow optimization. Familiarity with underwriting, claims, pricing, and relevant regulatory environments.
Preferred/Plus
RAG Expertise: Hands-on with vector databases and search (e.g., Vertex AI RAG Engine, OpenSearch, pgvector/Postgres), ANN indexing (HNSW), advanced rerankers (cross-encoders), and evaluation frameworks (RAGAS, TruLens, DeepEval) tailored for conversational AI in insurance.
Document AI Tooling: Practical experience with PyMuPDF/pdfplumber, Apache Tika; advanced OCR (Tesseract); layout-aware models (LayoutLM); and table extraction (Camelot/Tabula) for processing insurance documents.
Orchestration Frameworks: Familiarity with LangChain, LangGraph, or LlamaIndex; structured tool/function calling and guardrails for complex AI agents.
Embedding Model Selection: Experience comparing and selecting embedding models (OpenAI/Cohere/Voyage vs. open-source like bge/e5/gte) for domain-specific insurance corpora; understanding dimension/quality/cost/latency trade-offs.
Responsible AI \& Safety: Expertise in bias/fairness testing, hallucination mitigation, grounding checks, safety filters; and comprehensive model risk documentation for conversational AI.
Synthetic Data Techniques: Hands-on experience generating synthetic data using GenAI, including validation strategies and augmentation for low-frequency events in insurance.
Broader Modalities (Nice to Have): Experience with speech/vision/multimodal AI applications within an insurance context.
LLM Fine-tuning: Experience fine-tuning LLMs using PEFT/LoRA, and practical experience with model distillation.
Agile Experience: Experience working in an Agile framework with iterative delivery and continuous feedback loops.
Education, Experience, Certifications and Licenses
5 to 9 years of experience with Bachelor's degree. Less than 5 years accepted with Master's or Ph.D.
Preference for Master's or Ph.D. in Machine Learning, Applied Mathematics, Data Science, Computer Science, or a similar analytical field, or progress towards a relevant professional designation.
4\+ years of Python and SQL development experience.
1\+ years of experience in the insurance or broader financial services industry preferred.Candidate must be authorized to work in the US without company sponsorship. The company will not support the STEM OPT I-983 Training Plan endorsement for this position.

[Company] is where careers accelerate. We move fast, think boldly, and empower you to do the best work of your life. You’ll be surrounded by teammates who care deeply, challenge each other, and celebrate wins. With tools that amplify your impact and a culture that backs your ambition, you won’t just contribute. You’ll make things happen–fast.
[Company] is where careers accelerate. We move fast, think boldly, and empower you to do the best work of your life. You'll be surrounded by teammates who care deeply, challenge each other, and celebrate wins. With tools that amplify your impact and a culture that backs your ambition, you won't just contribute. You'll make things happen—fast.

The Opportunity

[Company] has rebuilt around AI. We have the orchestration infrastructure, the tools platform, the intelligence layer. Now we need someone to turn that infrastructure into outcomes—someone who lives at the intersection of what's technically possible and what GTM teams actually need.

This role owns the agentic ecosystem: the core agents that power [Company]'s products, the Plays library that packages intelligence into deployable workflows, and the marketplace where customers discover, customize, and deploy automation. You'll build agents that book meetings, enrich pipelines, resurrect dead leads, and orchestrate multi-step GTM motions. Then you'll package them into Plays that non-technical users can deploy in clicks, not code.

You're not managing from the sidelines. You're building. You'll work in our Plays builder the way a RevOps leader works in their automation tools—hands-on, iterative, constantly shipping. You'll prototype agents, refine prompts, test orchestration patterns, and validate that what we build actually moves pipeline. When you identify a gap in what our agents can do, you'll partner with the tools and infrastructure teams to close it.

The marketplace you own is how [Company]'s agentic capabilities reach the world. Internal teams deploy your Plays across Copilot, GTM Studio, and MarketingOS. Customers browse, customize, and activate automation from your library. Partners and developers connect through MCP gateways you define. Every surface where [Company]'s agentic intelligence becomes accessible—you own it.

This is a rare role: part builder, part GTM strategist, part platform owner. You need to understand how SDR teams actually prospect, how marketing ops builds nurture flows, how CS teams identify expansion signals. Then you need to translate that understanding into agents and Plays that work reliably at scale. The infrastructure exists. The data exists. We need someone to turn it into the workflows that transform how GTM teams operate.

What You'll Do

Build Core Agents & Plays
Design, build, and iterate on the agents and Plays that ship inside [Company]'s products. You'll work at multiple levels of abstraction: building agents from primitives when novel capabilities require it, composing existing agents into complex orchestrations, and packaging everything into Plays that non-technical users can deploy. This isn't spec-writing—you'll build in our Plays environment daily, testing what works and shipping what delivers.

Own the Plays Library
Curate and expand [Company]'s library of pre-built GTM workflows. Identify high-value use cases through customer research, competitive analysis, and GTM intuition. Prioritize which Plays to build, define their logic and orchestration, validate they deliver measurable outcomes, and iterate based on adoption and performance data. The library is the product—its breadth, quality, and relevance determine whether customers see [Company] as a data vendor or an automation platform.

Define the Marketplace Experience
Own the customer-facing marketplace where users discover, evaluate, and deploy agentic workflows. Define the information architecture, discovery mechanisms, and activation flows that help users find the right Play for their use case. Balance curation with abundance—enough options to address diverse needs, enough guidance to prevent paralysis. Measure success through activation rates, time-to-value, and Play retention.

Translate GTM Expertise into Agentic Design
The best agents encode deep understanding of the workflows they automate. Bring GTM operational expertise to agent design: How do top SDRs actually research accounts? What signals trigger effective outreach timing? How do marketing ops teams think about lead routing? Translate this knowledge into agent behaviors, Play logic, and orchestration patterns that feel like they were built by someone who's lived in RevOps.

Close the Loop with Platform Teams
You're the voice of "what agents need to do" speaking to the teams building "what agents can do." When you discover that a critical GTM workflow requires a capability that doesn't exist, partner with the tools and infrastructure teams to prioritize it. When platform improvements unlock new possibilities, translate them into Plays that demonstrate value. You're the bridge between infrastructure leverage and customer outcomes.

What You'll Bring

6-15 years product management experience with 2+ years focused on GTM technology, workflow automation, or AI-powered products

Direct experience building agentic workflows—you've shipped automation using tools like Relevance.ai, Lindy.ai, n8n, or similar no-code/low-code agent builders

Hands-on familiarity with AI-assisted development (Claude Code, Cursor, or similar)—you prototype and build, not just specify

Deep understanding of GTM operations: how sales, marketing, and success teams actually work, the tools they use, the metrics they track, the workflows they build

Experience with complex workflow orchestration—multi-step, multi-system automation with branching logic, error handling, and human-in-the-loop patterns

Track record shipping products where you personally built significant portions of the experience, not just managed engineers building it

Who You Are

Builder First
You don't just write requirements—you build. You're comfortable in no-code workflow builders, and you reach for Claude Code or Cursor when you need to go deeper. You prototype to learn, ship to validate, and iterate based on what you discover. Your PRDs include artifacts you've built, not just wireframes someone else will implement.

GTM Operations Native
You've lived in the world your users inhabit. You've built sequences in Outreach or Salesloft. You've configured lead routing in Salesforce or HubSpot. You've debugged why a Marketo program isn't firing. You understand the gap between how GTM workflows should work and how they actually work—the edge cases, the data quality issues, the human workarounds. This knowledge shapes every agent you design.

Agentic Workflow Intuition
You understand what makes agents succeed or fail. You know how to write tool descriptions that guide reliable invocation. You've debugged orchestration flows where agents took unexpected paths. You think about failure modes, human oversight, and graceful degradation. You can look at a complex workflow and identify where it will break—then design around it.

Marketplace Product Sense
You've thought about discovery, curation, and activation at scale. You understand that a library of 500 Plays is worthless if users can't find the right one. You think about information architecture, progressive disclosure, and the journey from "browsing" to "deployed and delivering value." You balance flexibility with guidance.

Customer Empathy with Technical Depth
You can sit with a VP of Sales and understand their pipeline challenges, then translate that into agent specifications an ML engineer can build. You move fluidly between "we need to accelerate deal velocity" and "the agent needs to invoke the CRM lookup tool before the enrichment tool to avoid redundant API calls." You don't choose between business context and technical precision—you hold both.

Intellectual Curiosity
The agentic landscape evolves weekly. You follow what's shipping—new agent frameworks, orchestration patterns, MCP developments, prompt engineering techniques. You try new tools not because it's your job but because you're genuinely curious about what's possible. You bring external learnings back to the team and challenge assumptions when better approaches emerge.

Why choose us?

Are you ready to take the next step in your career? Join us for an exciting opportunity at Albertsons Companies, where innovation and customer service go hand-in-hand!

At Albertsons Companies, we are looking for someone who’s not just seeking a job, but someone who wants to make an impact. In this role, you’ll have the opportunity to lead, innovate, and contribute to the growth of a company that values great service and lasting customer relationships. This position offers the chance to work in a fast-paced, dynamic environment that’s constantly evolving.

Main responsibilities:

As a Product Manager focused on Retail Operations and Data Products, you will work at the intersection of retail operations, data, and technology to drive measurable improvements in operational performance. You will partner closely with business, operations, technology, data science, and other product managers to translate operational needs into scalable, data-driven solutions. You will define how operational metrics are structured and surfaced, enabling leaders to quickly identify issues, understand drivers, and take action. You will operate as a team player, collaborating cross-functionally and influencing alignment across teams. Your ability to break down complex problems and convert data into clear, actionable insights will play a critical role in enabling better decision-making and execution across retail operations. This role requires a team player with a high capacity to work in ambiguity, uncover opportunities, ownership and lead with clarity and confidence. If you’re looking to play a part in transforming retail operations through data and technology, come join us!

* Retail Operations Problem Solving: Dive into retail workflows such as inventory management, shrink reduction, product handling, and store execution to understand pain points, identify gaps, and define solutions that improve operational performance
* Product Ownership: Own delivery of defined product components from concept through launch, working closely with senior product managers to ensure alignment with overall product direction and business goals
* Product Definition: Translate retail operations needs into clear product requirements, define scope and success criteria, and prioritize solutions that drive measurable operational impact
* Roadmap Contribution: Contribute to product roadmap and prioritization by aligning initiatives with retail operations priorities such as inventory accuracy, shrink and execution consistency
* Product Research: Analyze operational metrics, stakeholder inputs, and field feedback to inform product direction and ensure solutions are grounded in real-world retail challenges
* Cross-Functional Partnership: Work closely with business, retail operations, engineering, and data teams to refine solutions, resolve dependencies, and drive execution
* Stakeholder Alignment: Maintain alignment across stakeholders by communicating progress, gathering feedback, and ensuring solutions are practical and usable, working closely with senior product managers to stay aligned on direction and priorities
* Operational Impact \& Iteration: Monitor product performance, incorporate feedback from store and business teams, and continuously refine solutions to improve outcomes
* Operate in Ambiguity: Navigate evolving priorities, ask the right questions, and bring structure to complex operational problem spaces
* Operational Responsibilities: Contribute to product development lifecycle activities including:
+ Leading product discussions and participating in kickoff sessions with business and engineering teams
+ Translating product direction into clear and actionable requirements for development
+ Ensuring features align with defined objectives and operational needs
+ Defining clear acceptance criteria and success measures for delivered solutions
+ Identifying dependencies and coordinating across related initiatives

We are looking for candidates who possess the following:

* Bachelor’s degree in Engineering, Business, or a related field.
* 3–5 years of experience in Product Management, Data Analytics, or a related role.
* Strong analytical skills with experience working with data to support decision-making.
* Experience translating business or operational needs into product solutions or requirements.
* Familiarity with product and data tools and querying languages (e.g., SQL, BI tools, Jira)
* Experience building or contributing to dashboards, reporting, or data-driven products
* Ability to work cross-functionally and communicate effectively with business and technical teams
* Proven ability to manage multiple priorities and deliver in fast-paced, ambiguous environments.

We believe the successful candidate has these qualifications and experience:

* Exposure to retail operations, supply chain, or store-facing processes.
* Retail, eCommerce, or supply chain experience.
* Exceptional ability to operate as a team player and build strong relationships across teams.
* Demonstrated ability to influence stakeholders and communicate complex ideas effectively.
* Passionate about leveraging data and technology to improve retail operations.

We also provide a variety of benefits including:

* Competitive wages paid weekly
* Associate discounts
* Time off (vacation, holidays, sick pay). For eligibility requirements please visit myACI Benefits
* Leaders invested in your training, career growth and development
* An inclusive work environment with talented colleagues who reflect the communities we serve

The team works to ensure that these highly technical systems are usable, intuitive, and aligned with real world operational needs.

What you’ll need

Genomics Data Scientist

Location: Pleasanton, CA

About the Role:
We are seeking a motivated Genomics Data Scientist to join our multidisciplinary team focused on advancing single-cell and spatial genomics technologies. In this role, you will leverage your expertise in computational biology, statistical modeling, and large-scale data analysis to extract biological insights from complex high-throughput sequencing datasets. You will collaborate closely with wet-lab scientists and software engineers to develop novel algorithms and analytical pipelines that enhance our understanding of cellular heterogeneity and disease mechanisms. This position offers the opportunity to contribute to cutting-edge innovations in genomics and precision medicine within a fast-paced biotech environment.

Responsibilities:
- Analyze and interpret large-scale single-cell RNA-seq, ATAC-seq, and spatial transcriptomics datasets using advanced statistical and machine learning techniques
- Develop, optimize, and maintain reproducible computational pipelines for data processing, quality control, normalization, and downstream analysis
- Collaborate with cross-functional teams including biologists, product managers, and engineers to design experiments and integrate multi-omics data for comprehensive biological insights
- Implement and benchmark novel algorithms for dimensionality reduction, clustering, cell type annotation, trajectory inference, and differential expression analysis
- Present data-driven findings and technical solutions clearly to both technical and non-technical stakeholders through reports, visualizations, and presentations
- Stay current with emerging bioinformatics tools, single-cell genomics methodologies, and computational frameworks to continuously improve analytical approaches
- Contribute to the development of internal software tools and participate in code reviews to maintain high-quality, scalable, and well-documented codebases

Requirements:
- Master’s degree or PhD in Bioinformatics, Computational Biology, Computer Science, Statistics, or related quantitative field
- 2-5 years of hands-on experience analyzing high-dimensional genomics data, preferably single-cell or spatial omics datasets
- Proficiency in Python and R, including libraries such as Scanpy, Seurat, Bioconductor, and scikit-learn for data analysis and visualization
- Strong background in statistical modeling, machine learning, and algorithm development tailored to biological data
- Experience working with cloud computing platforms (AWS, Google Cloud, or Azure) and containerization technologies such as Docker and Kubernetes
- Familiarity with data formats and standards in genomics (FASTQ, BAM, HDF5, AnnData) and experience with relevant workflows (Nextflow, Snakemake)
- Excellent communication skills with the ability to translate complex computational results into actionable biological insights
- Proven ability to work collaboratively in interdisciplinary teams within a fast-paced, innovative environment

Nice to Have:
- Experience with C++ or Julia for performance-critical algorithm implementation
- Prior exposure to genomics assay development or wet-lab experimental design
- Knowledge of regulatory requirements and data privacy standards related to clinical genomics data
- Publications or contributions to open-source bioinformatics projects

We are looking for talented engineers who combine strong communication, stakeholder management, and hands-on expertise in AI agents. A sense of ownership, continuous learning, and genuine curiosity about the rapidly evolving agent ecosystem are essential.

At [Company] AI, you will design, build, and deploy production-grade Agentic AI solutions for large enterprise clients. We are a multi-cloud (AWS, Azure, GCP) and multi-LLM (OpenAI, Azure OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini) AI solutions firm, and you will work with [Company] teams across the full development lifecycle, from discovery to production.

* Partner with client stakeholders, product owners, and business leads to understand requirements, shape solutions, and communicate progress clearly and credibly

Qualifications

Why [Company] AI

[Company] AI is a multi-cloud, multi-LLM AI solutions firm delivering enterprise AI to Fortune 500 clients. You will work directly with senior engineering and client teams, take ownership of meaningful features, and grow quickly in a high-trust, high-accountability environment.

Senior Operations Research Analyst

Location: Chicago, IL

About the Role:
We are seeking a Senior Operations Research Analyst with deep expertise in leveraging advanced analytical methods to optimize manufacturing and supply chain operations. In this role, you will be instrumental in driving data-driven decision-making across complex logistics and production processes, helping to enhance operational efficiency, reduce costs, and improve service levels. You will collaborate closely with cross-functional teams including supply chain planners, data engineers, and software developers to build scalable optimization models and simulation tools that address real-world manufacturing challenges. The ideal candidate combines strong mathematical modeling skills with practical industry knowledge and is comfortable working with large datasets and modern analytics platforms.

Responsibilities:
- Develop and implement advanced operations research models including linear programming, mixed-integer programming, simulation, and stochastic optimization to optimize manufacturing scheduling, inventory management, and transportation logistics.
- Translate business problems into quantitative models and deliver actionable insights that influence strategic and tactical decision-making.
- Collaborate with data engineering and software development teams to integrate optimization algorithms into cloud-based platforms and APIs.
- Conduct scenario analysis and sensitivity testing to evaluate the impact of various operational strategies and uncertainties.
- Utilize statistical analysis and machine learning techniques to forecast demand and identify patterns that affect production efficiency.
- Communicate complex analytical findings effectively to both technical and non-technical stakeholders through clear visualizations and presentations.
- Stay current with emerging research and technologies in operations research and supply chain analytics, recommending innovative solutions for continuous improvement.
- Mentor junior analysts, providing guidance on modeling approaches, coding best practices, and problem-solving techniques.

Requirements:
- Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Operations Research, Industrial Engineering, Applied Mathematics, or a related quantitative discipline.
- 5-10 years of professional experience applying operations research methods in manufacturing, supply chain management, or logistics.
- Proficiency in optimization software and programming languages such as Python (with libraries like PuLP, Pyomo), R, Gurobi, CPLEX, or similar solvers.
- Strong experience with simulation tools such as AnyLogic, Arena, or Simio is highly preferred.
- Expertise in data manipulation and analysis using SQL and data visualization tools such as Tableau or Power BI.
- Familiarity with cloud computing platforms (AWS, Azure, or Google Cloud) and deploying models in production environments.
- Solid understanding of lean manufacturing principles, Six Sigma methodologies, and supply chain frameworks.
- Excellent problem-solving skills with a track record of delivering scalable, high-impact solutions in a fast-paced environment.

Nice to Have:
- Experience with advanced machine learning frameworks (TensorFlow, Scikit-learn) applied to demand forecasting or predictive maintenance.
- Knowledge of transportation management systems (TMS) and warehouse management systems (WMS).
- Certification in Lean Six Sigma (Green Belt or Black Belt) or Certified Analytics Professional (CAP).
- Prior exposure to agile software development methodologies and working within cross-functional DevOps teams.

We're looking for a Revenue Enablement Manager for our Customer Office to own the enablement experience for [Company]'s post-sale organization including Customer Success Managers, Renewal Owners, and the broader customer-facing teams responsible for retention, expansion, and long-term value delivery. This role sits within the Revenue Enablement function and is dedicated to building the capabilities, frameworks, and learning systems that drive net revenue retention.

This position operates at the intersection of customer success strategy and field execution, ensuring that CSMs and renewal owners have the knowledge, tools, and skills to deepen customer relationships, demonstrate ROI, and grow accounts.

This role is open in USA and Canada in provinces and states we can legally hire in and will report into the Chief of Staff, Revenue.

WHAT YOU’LL DO

Develop and maintain customer success and renewal motion playbooks, strategic business review (SBR) guides, customer office management framework and expansion play materials in close collaboration with CS Leadership, Customer Experience & Strategy, Product Marketing, and Revenue Operations.

Build a coaching cadence for the customer office, leveraging AI conversation intelligence and customer health data to identify skill gaps, improve discovery and value-articulation skills, and increase commercial confidence among CSMs and renewal owners.

Translate [Company]'s value proposition into assets and internal training that equip the post-sale team to demonstrate ROI, connect usage to business outcomes, and tell a compelling story at renewal and expansion.

Design and deliver enablement programs to ensure the post-sale team is fully prepared ahead of any change that affects how they show up with customers.

Partner with the Revenue Operations and Customer Experience & Strategy teams to drive adoption of CS tools, health scoring systems, and renewal workflows. Turn process and system changes into clear enablement experiences that reduce friction and increase consistency across the team.

Build a continuous learning program for CSMs and renewal owners through initiatives that reinforce value-based engagement, commercial acumen, and multi-threaded relationship building.

Establish and track enablement metrics tied to post-sale performance, such as NRR, expansion pipeline, renewal conversion rates, and program adoption, iterating based on field feedback and CS leadership input.

Serve as an active member of the Revenue Enablement Council, representing the post-sale voice and ensuring customer office initiatives are properly coordinated and sequenced across the broader GTM organization.

WHAT YOU’LL NEED

8+ years of experience in customer success enablement, CS operations, or a quota-carrying CSM/account management role within a B2B SaaS company. Background in consulting or strategic advisory is a major plus.

Demonstrated experience building and delivering enablement programs with measurable impact on NRR, renewal rates, or expansion pipeline

Deep understanding of the customer success motion  health scoring, QBR/EBR frameworks, value realization methodologies, and renewal commercial negotiation

Experience with AI-powered customer success tools, including conversational intelligence, AI-assisted health scoring, and automated coaching systems with a practical understanding of how to embed these capabilities into CSM workflows to improve retention outcomes and commercial effectiveness.

Familiarity with CS platforms (e.g., Gainsight, Salesforce, Gong, and LMS tools)

Experience working cross-functionally with CS Leadership, Product Marketing, RevOps, and Finance to build renewal and expansion programs that are both commercially rigorous and customer-centric

Strong facilitation and communication skills. Effective in designing async learning and running live workshops with experienced CSMs and commercial-focused renewal teams

Ability to translate complex product value into clear, customer-facing narratives and internal training that builds commercial confidence across the post-sale team

WHO YOU ARE

Solution seeker: You’re focused on tackling new challenges, solving problems, and moving the business forward—and you don’t wait to be asked.

Lifelong learner: You have a growth mindset – you’re here to learn, experiment, seek, apply, and provide feedback, share what works with your team, and move on from what doesn’t.

Resilient adapter: In the face of change and challenges, you bring a thoughtful, calm approach, and a focus on finding the new opportunity.

Intentional collaborator. You build positive working relationships across the business, bringing people together to foster new opportunities and to facilitate the efficient flow of information.

Critical challenger: You have the trust in your team to ask difficult questions in order to get to the best end result.

Active communicator: You listen actively and communicate ideas and information clearly, inclusively, and proactively.

Integrated thinker: You look beyond your role and responsibilities to understand how your team’s work drives broader organizational goals.

Accountable owner: You take pride in the work you’re responsible for with a mindset of ultimate accountability and reliability for the outcomes.

Bar-raiser: You step up to help your team grow and succeed, even when that means going beyond what might be expected.

In all we do, our six guiding principles light the way:
Step Up:

One Team:
Make [Company] a place where everyone feels safe, welcome, valued, and empowered to do their best  work without compromising who they are. OneTeam FreeToBeMe

Go Fast, Be Agile:

Play to Win:

About the Role
We are seeking a Senior Clinical Data Scientist to join our dynamic team focused on transforming oncology care through real-world evidence and advanced analytics. In this role, you will leverage your deep expertise in clinical data science, machine learning, and biostatistics to generate actionable insights from complex healthcare datasets. You will collaborate closely with cross-functional teams including clinical operations, epidemiology, and software engineering to design and implement scalable analytic solutions that drive evidence generation and support clinical decision-making. This position requires a strong foundation in clinical data management, statistical modeling, and experience working with electronic health records (EHR) and real-world data (RWD) in oncology or related therapeutic areas.

CRM \& Lifecycle Marketing Manager
======================================

Requisition ID: 16530
Location:
Chicago, IL, US, 60631
Pay Type: Salary
Position type: Full-time / Salary
Join [Company] as a CRM \& Lifecycle Marketing Manager and help construct what's next. If you're ready to put your skills to work on projects that matter — and build a career with a company that’s building North America — we want to hear from you!

ABOUT THE ROLE

We are seeking a data-driven, strategic Marketing Manager to lead our customer acquisition and retention efforts. This role is built for a CRM expert who lives and breathes lead lifecycle management. You will be responsible for building the bridge between marketing curiosity and sales conversion by leveraging Salesforce and Marketing Cloud to create sophisticated, automated customer journeys.

The ideal candidate doesn't just "send emails", they build ecosystems for long-term impact. You will define how we identify high-value prospects, how we nurture them through the funnel, and how we measure success at every touchpoint.

WHAT YOU'LL ACCOMPLISH

* Lead Generation \& Strategy: Develop and execute multi-channel lead generation campaigns to fuel the sales pipeline, ensuring a steady flow of high-quality prospects.
* CRM Management (Salesforce): Act as the marketing lead for our Salesforce instance. Oversee data hygiene, lead routing rules, and the integration between sales and marketing activities.
* Marketing Automation (Marketing Cloud): Design, build, and deploy automated journeys in Salesforce Marketing Cloud, including welcome series, re-engagement campaigns, and cross-selling. Optimize platform capabilities for segmentation, personalization and performance.
* Campaign Execution \& Schedule Management: Manage the cross-market email marketing calendar for customer and prospect communications. Oversee campaign development, scheduling, QA, launch, and mobile optimization.
* Lead Scoring \& Optimization: Develop and refine our lead scoring models to prioritize high-intent prospects, ensuring the Sales team focuses their energy on the most impactful leads.
* Lead Nurturing: Create personalized, high-touch nurturing workflows that move leads from awareness to conversion.
* Performance Analytics: Monitor and report on key performance indicators (KPIs) such as MQL-to-SQL conversion rates, email engagement metrics, and overall ROI of lifecycle campaigns.
* Cross-Functional Collaboration: Work closely with our cement and ACM commercial organizations, IT commercial teams, marketing and enterprise teams to align messaging and strategic priorities.
* Demonstrate a commitment to communicating, improving and adhering to health, safety and environmental policies in all work environments and areas. Promote a culture of safety and exhibit these behaviors.

WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

Education: Bachelor's degree or 8 years of equivalent experience

Required Work Experience:

* 8\+ years of experience in B2B or high-growth B2C marketing, with at least 2 years in a CRM lead role.
* Proven ability to map out a customer journey from first touch to final sale.
* Deep understanding of lead scoring logic, behavioral triggers, and A/B testing methodologies.
* You enjoy "nerding out" on data. You use metrics to tell a story and justify budget spend or tactical shifts.
* Exceptional writing skills for crafting compelling nurture copy and the ability to present complex data to executive leadership.
* Ability to set strategic and campaign-level KPIs, develop and implement performance measurement tools, understanding of and translate data into actionable recommendations.

Required Technical Skills: Advanced proficiency in Salesforce CRM and Salesforce Pardot/Marketing Cloud is required. Experience with automation platforms like Zapier is a plus. Certification (e.g., Pardot, Marketing Cloud Email Specialist or Consultant) is a major plus.

Travel Requirements: 25-40%

Additional Requirements:

* Position is in a high-volume, rapidly paced environment that requires problem solving, analytical and verbal/written communication skills, so an ideal candidate will have strong experience leading both strategic development and tactical execution.
* Strong understanding of deliverability best practices and campaign performance optimization.
* Strong project management experience with complex, multi-step programs, multiple stakeholders and competing priorities
* Self-starter with a strong work ethic and equal ability to work collaboratively with a team, cross-functionally, and with senior leadership
* Comfort in leveraging analytics and performance metrics and provide guidance to the team to make decisions
* Successful candidates must adhere to all safety protocols and proper use of [Company] approved Personal Protection Equipment ("PPE"), including but not limited to respirators. Subject to applicable law, employees that are required to wear respirators must be clean shaven where the respirator seal meets the face in order to pass the qualitative and quantitative fit tests.

* Educational Assistance Program
* Dress for your day

BUILDING INCLUSIVE WORKSPACES

At [Company], there is endless opportunity for you to play your part. Whether you’re in a technical, managerial, or frontline role, you can shape a career that works for you. We're seeking builders, creative thinkers and innovators. Come put your expertise to work while developing the knowledge and skills to drive your career forward. With us you’ll have the chance to build your ambition!
